title: CollisionSpike Dataverse security role (authoring spec)
description: Reviewable, offline authoring shape for ONE Dataverse security role (Phase 9 / ADR-0017 G8 least-privilege model). NOT a Dataverse import file -- 28-roles.ps1 translates this into a `roles` record + AddPrivilegesRole action calls via the Web API at [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN]. The privilege GUIDs and the business-unit GUID are ENVIRONMENT-RESOLVED (the apply script looks them up by name at runtime, exactly as 01-choicesets / optionset-ids.json handle choiceset GUIDs); this file only declares what is STABLE: table logical names, the eight table-privilege axes, and a named access-level depth per axis.

roleName.description: Display name of the security role as it appears in the env (e.g. 'CollisionSpike User').

schemaName.description: Stable internal id used by 28-roles.ps1 to find-or-create the role idempotently (matched on `name`).

supersetOf.description: Optional: the schemaName of the role this one is a superset of (Admin = User + extra). Documentation only -- the matrix below is always FULL and self-contained (the script never merges).

state.description: built-gated-off = role is created live by 28-roles.ps1 but NEVER assigned (operator assigns at activation). deferred = documented, not built.

assignment.description: GATED-OFF SEMANTICS: the build CREATES the role; it never assigns it to a user/team. Assignment is the operator's activation step.

resolve.description: ENVIRONMENT-RESOLVED. A role must bind to a business unit; we bind to the environment's ROOT business unit (parentbusinessunitid eq null). The GUID is looked up at apply time -- it is NOT stored here (it differs per environment), mirroring optionset-ids.json for choiceset GUIDs.

summary.description: One-line description of the role's intent (least-privilege rationale).

appliesTo.description: Who holds this role in real life (e.g. 'day-to-day intake staff').

tablePrivileges.description: One entry per cr1bd_* table the role grants on. ABSENCE of a table = NO privilege of any kind on it (least-privilege default-deny).

table.description: Logical name of the table the privileges apply to.

rationale.description: Why this table gets these depths (least-privilege justification).

privileges.description: The 8 table-privilege axes. Each value is a named ACCESS LEVEL (depth). Omit an axis (or set 'None') to grant nothing on it. Named levels map to Web API PrivilegeDepth: None=(no privilege), User=Basic(0), BusinessUnit=Local(1), ParentChild=Deep(2), Organization=Global(3).

miscPrivileges.description: Optional NON-table (task-based / platform) privileges granted by stable privilege NAME (e.g. prvActOnBehalfOfAnotherUser, environment-variable management privileges). GUID is environment-resolved by 28-roles.ps1; depth named as below. Use sparingly -- the bulk of the model is table privileges.

privilegeName.description: Stable privilege `name` (e.g. prvReadEnvironmentVariableDefinition). Looked up to a GUID at apply time.

accessLevel.description: Named access level. None = grant no privilege on this axis. User=Basic(0), BusinessUnit=Local(1), ParentChild=Deep(2), Organization=Global(3) -- the integer is the Web API PrivilegeDepth the apply script sends.
